qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated terminal WebSocket access permits command execution in edit mode
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same vulnerable `/terminal/ws` endpoint in `marimo/_server/api/endpoints/terminal.py` and the same missing `validate_auth()` or equivalent permission guard before `websocket.accept()` and `pty.fork()` that the advisory describes.

selected_finding.title: Unauthenticated terminal WebSocket access permits command execution in edit mode

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's exact root cause and its copied PoC demonstrated unauthenticated PTY shell command execution through `/terminal/ws` while authentication still blocked the normal `/ws` WebSocket.
evidence: The advisory and the finding both describe `marimo/_server/api/endpoints/terminal.py` accepting `/terminal/ws` connections without `validate_auth()` and then spawning a PTY shell.

The copied PoC run in `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_1913fc91/.judge/poc_runs/unauthenticated-terminal-websocket-rce/run.log` printed `[evohunt] unauthenticated /ws correctly rejected`, showing auth was enabled and enforced on the main notebook WebSocket.

The same run log then printed `[evohunt] unauthenticated /terminal/ws executed shell command`, showed a root shell prompt, and returned the proof string `AUTH_BYPASS_13` after executing `echo AUTH_BYPASS_$(expr 6 + 7)` over the unauthenticated terminal WebSocket.

poc_executions.observed_effect: With auth enabled, unauthenticated `/ws` access was rejected, but an unauthenticated `/terminal/ws` connection spawned a PTY shell as `root` and executed `echo AUTH_BYPASS_$(expr 6 + 7)`, returning `AUTH_BYPASS_13` in the runtime log.
